section_name: Residential parking system in the village of Pelham in the county of Westchester
article_title: Regul. of Traffic By Cities & Villages

subsections.text: Notwithstanding the provisions of any law to the contrary, the village board of the village of Pelham may, by adoption of a local law or ordinance, provide for a residential parking permit system and fix and require the payment of fees applicable to parking within the area in which such parking system is in effect in accordance with the provisions of this section.
Such residential parking permit system may only be established within the area of the village consisting of the following roadways:

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: the east side of Wolfs Lane, from Franklin Place to Boulevard;

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: the south side of Boulevard West, from Wolfs Lane to the city of Mount Vernon boundary;

subsections.label: c
subsections.text: both sides of Fifth Avenue, from Sixth Street to Willow Avenue;

subsections.label: d
subsections.text: both sides of NYAC Avenue, from Franklin Avenue to Second Street;

subsections.label: e
subsections.text: both sides of Corlies Avenue, from Colonial Avenue to Second Street;

subsections.label: f
subsections.text: both sides of Pelhamdale Avenue, from Colonial Avenue to Irving Place;

subsections.label: g
subsections.text: both sides of Highbrook Avenue, from Colonial Avenue to Irving Place;

subsections.label: h
subsections.text: both sides of Boulevard, from Wolfs Lane to Highbrook Avenue;

subsections.label: i
subsections.text: both sides of Seventh Street, from the west end of Seventh Street (dead end) to Sixth Avenue;

subsections.label: j
subsections.text: both sides of Fourth Avenue, from Seventh Street to the dead end;

subsections.label: k
subsections.text: both sides of Harmon Avenue, from Fifth Avenue to Harmon Place;

subsections.label: l
subsections.text: the north side of First Street, from Wolfs Lane to Corlies Avenue;

subsections.label: m
subsections.text: the west side of NYAC Avenue, two hundred feet south of the intersection with First Street to East Second Street;

subsections.label: n
subsections.text: the east side of Fourth Avenue, from First Street to Second Street;

subsections.label: o
subsections.text: the west side of First Avenue, from First Street to Third Street.
Notwithstanding the foregoing, no permit shall be required on those portions of such streets where the adjacent properties are zoned for commercial/retail use.
The local law or ordinance providing for such residential parking system shall:

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: set forth factors necessitating the enactment of such parking system;

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: provide that motor vehicles registered pursuant to § 404-A (Registration of motor vehicles of severely disabled persons) shall be exempt from any permit requirement;

subsections.label: c
subsections.text: provide the times of the day and days of the week during which permit requirements shall be in effect;

subsections.label: d
subsections.text: make not less than twenty percent of the spaces within the permit area available to nonresidents, and shall provide short term parking of not less than one hundred twenty minutes in duration in the permit area;

subsections.label: e
subsections.text: provide the schedule of fees to be paid for such permits; and

subsections.label: f
subsections.text: provide that such fees shall be credited to the general fund of the village unless otherwise specified in such local law.

No ordinance shall be adopted pursuant to this section until a public hearing thereon has been had in the same manner as required for public hearings on a local law pursuant to the municipal home rule law.
